primary_topics: Venn diagram framework for weld cracking control
cases_referenced: Seawolf submarine welding (blue jelly suits, 400°F preheat)
references_made: Stout & Doty, Weldability of Steels (cookbook reference)
tom_slips_bracketed: welding engineers welding society → [American] Welding Society
stoughton dodie → Stout and Doty [Stout & Doty]
captioner_errors_corrected_silently: asme boiler brushed your method code → ASME Boiler Pressure Vessel Code

notes: Short lecture segment (~9 minutes) covering three levels of preheat (>50°F, 100-300°F, >300°F) and the rationale for each in terms of moisture removal, hydrogen diffusion, and tempering. Tom introduces his own Venn diagram framework (stress × hardness × hydrogen) and notes this explanation is original to him. Session ends with promise to continue preheat/post-heat next day.
